title: Function components

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Function components; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Learn and Reference; React 19.2; provisions applicable to Function components
sources.claim: A function component receives props and returns renderable React nodes while remaining pure during render.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A function component receives props and returns renderable React nodes while remaining pure during render.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Greeting receives name and returns one heading without mutating props or external state. Your goal is to extract and test a cohesive component without hidden mutation. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Declarative UI and re-rendering; html semantics; javascript functions forms. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A function component receives props and returns renderable React nodes while remaining pure during render. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Components can render null, hooks require stable call order, and local variables reset on each render. This draft therefore rejects: A component function runs once when mounted and retains its local variables forever.

includes: Define capitalized pure component functions with a single responsibility.
Extract and test a cohesive component without hidden mutation.
excludes: Components can render null, hooks require stable call order, and local variables reset on each render.

outcomes: Extract and test a cohesive component without hidden mutation.

examples.title: Function components: concrete case
examples.situation: Greeting receives name and returns one heading without mutating props or external state.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: Greeting receives name and returns one heading without mutating props or external state.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Component functions can run repeatedly; persist changing UI data in state or refs, not ordinary render locals.
examples.result: The result follows because Component functions can run repeatedly; persist changing UI data in state or refs, not ordinary render locals.

examples.title: Function components: boundary case
examples.situation: Components can render null, hooks require stable call order, and local variables reset on each render.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Components can render null, hooks require stable call order, and local variables reset on each render.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: A component function runs once when mounted and retains its local variables forever.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Component functions can run repeatedly; persist changing UI data in state or refs, not ordinary render locals.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A component function runs once when mounted and retains its local variables forever.
misconceptions.correction: Component functions can run repeatedly; persist changing UI data in state or refs, not ordinary render locals.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete React: Component-Based Browser Applications artifact to explain why this belief fails: A component function runs once when mounted and retains its local variables forever.

analogy.example: A state-driven component workshop can model Function components, where inputs and state produce a UI description.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Function components before using exact platform terms
limitations: A workshop does not reproduce reconciliation identity, hook ordering, concurrent rendering, effects, or browser accessibility APIs.
